1.description: Model solution. This figure shows the model solution using the calibrated parameters from TableII. All units in the vertical axes are in decimals, at quarterly terms. “Risk sharing” (bottom-left panel) is expression (15). “Credit/Total equity” (bottom-middle panel) is the aggregate amount of debt issued by the less risk-averse investors divided by the aggregate amount of wealth. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Real yield curve. Panel A displays the real yield curve in the model for three different levels ofx. The blue (gray) line represents the real yield curve whenxis two standard deviations below (above) its mean. The red line represents the real yield curve whenxis at its unconditional mean. Data are from TableI, Full Sample. Panel B displays the expected change in the short-term rate,μr$\mu _{r}$, and the diffusion for the short-term rate,σr$\sigma _{r}$. The vertical axis in Panel B is expressed in decimals at quarterly frequency. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Term premiums in the model.−covtP(dmtmt,dPt(T)Pt(T))$-\text{cov}_{t}^{\mathbb {P}}(\frac{dm_{t}}{m_{t}},\frac{dP_{t}^{(T) }}{P_{t}^{(T) }})$. The left panel shows−covtP(dmtmt,dPt(T)Pt(T))$-\text{cov}_{t}^{\mathbb {P}}(\frac{dm_{t}}{m_{t}},\frac{dP_{t}^{(T) }}{P_{t}^{(T) }})$across the state space. The right panel shows the mean covariance across maturities. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: The role ofψB${\bm {\psi_{B}}}$. The horizontal axis in all panels represents different values ofψB${\psi_{B}}$. The upper-left panel shows the unconditional derivative ofr(x)$r(x)$with respect tox. The upper-right panel displays the average slope 80-minus-4 quarters of the real yield curve. The bottom-left panel shows the approximation ofrx$r_{x}$using the example in equation (29) (the vertical axis in the lower-left panel is truncated at −0.5 to ease exposition). The bottom-right panel shows the price of risk. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Nominal term structure: Exogenous inflation case. The left panel plots the yield curve for three different values of π in solid red, dotted blue, and dashed gray while settingxat its unconditional mean. The right panel shows the same as the left panel, but sets π to its unconditional mean and plots the yield curve for three different values ofx. Data for nominal yields are from TableI, Full Sample. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Decomposition of 80-quarters nominal term premium. This figure shows the real component in the decomposition of an 80-quarter nominal bond term premium (shown in (23)). The nominal component, not shown, is given as 100 minus the real component. The left-hand panel displays the real components over thexdimension. The three lines represent different levels for the other state variable, π. The solid red line corresponds to π set to its unconditional mean; the dotted blue (dashed gray) line corresponds to π set to two standard deviations below (above) the steady state. The right-hand panel displays the real components over the π dimension. The three lines represent different levels for the other state variable,x. The solid red line is whenxis set to its unconditional mean; the dotted blue (dashed gray) line is whenxis set to two standard deviations below (above) the steady-state level. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Nominal term structure: Endogenous inflation case. The top panel shows the nominal term structure in the endogenous inflation case. The solid red line is whenxis set to the steady state; the dotted blue (dashed gray) line is whenxis set to standard deviations below (above) the mean. The lower-left panel shows the mean and volatility of inflation for different Taylor coefficientsδπ$\delta _{\pi }$. The lower-right panel shows the slope of the nominal term structure for different Taylor coefficientsδπ$\delta _{\pi }$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Decomposition of yields' shocks and inflation variance ratio. The top panels show the decomposition of shocks to nominal yields in the endogenous and exogenous inflation models, following Duffee (2018). The blue o's represent the standard deviations of yield news, the red x's the standard deviation of news attributed to expected inflation, and the solid black line the standard deviation of news attributed to real rates. The lower-left panel displays the variance ratio in the endogenous inflation model for different Taylor coefficientsδπ$\delta _{\pi }$(in solid blue, dotted red, and dashed gray), and the lower-right panel displays the variance ratio in the exogenous inflation model for different correlation coefficients ϕ13(in solid blue, dotted red, and dashed gray). The variance ratio is the unconditional variance of shocks to inflation news divided by the unconditional variance of yield news (see Duffee (2018), equation (7)). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Campbell-Shiller regressions. This figure shows the coefficients from Campbell and Shiller (1991) regressions. The left panels show the results for the “long-rate regression” (expression (31)) for the real rate (top left), for the nominal rate in the endogenous inflation model (middle left), and for the nominal rate in the exogenous inflation model (bottom left). The right panels show the estimated betas for the “short-rate regression” (expression (32)), for the real rate (top right), for the nominal rate in the endogenous inflation model (middle right), and for the nominal rate in the exogenous inflation model (bottom right). I use the full sample for nominal data and the 1999 to 2016 period for real rates due to lack of contiguous maturities in the full sample. The black bracket lines represent robust 95% confidence intervals in the empirical estimates. The coefficients in the model are population values. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

10.description: Model-implied paths. This figure shows the model-implied paths and their counterparts in the data. I obtain the model-implied series by feeding the model with the sequence of macro shocks shown in theAppendix, starting the model at the unconditional mean ofxin 1971:Q2. The upper panels show the 10-year nominal and real yields. The bottom-left panel shows the volatility of a 10-year nominal bond, which is computed in the data as the (annualized) quarterly standard deviation of daily changes in a 10-year nominal bond, and in the model as the (annualized) diffusion component of a 10-year nominal bond. The bottom-right panel shows the realized excess return on a 10-year nominal bond, which is computed as the realized holding-period return on a 10-year nominal bond both in the data and in the model (i.e., buy a 10-year bond in a given quarter and sell it as a nine-year bond one year later, minus the one-year rate in a given quarter). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
